title: narcissus asturiensis subsp. villarvildensis
intro: Narcissus asturiensis subsp. villarvildensis, also known as the Asturian narcissus or wild daffodil, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Amaryllidaceae family, native to the northern Iberian Peninsula. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, such as reducing anxiety and improving respiratory health, due to its active compounds like galantamine and lycorine. In herbal preparations, the bulbs of Narcissus asturiensis subsp. villarvildensis are often used to create infusions and tinctures, although its use should be carefully considered due to potential toxicity and side effects.
